name: QUO Inbound Source Contract
authority: G0/R0 design and local evidence only
purpose: Define the implementation-ready QUO inbound source contract without requiring QUO API discovery or any live source/M365 work.

allowed: Map future events to CRM - New Signals.
Define evidence, retention, redaction, approval, disable, revoke, and owner expectations.
Define synthetic fixture shapes with fake data only.
blocked: Inspect, request, store, or infer QUO secrets.
Process real phone, SMS, voicemail, or call traffic.
Send outbound SMS, callback, reply, or QUO API messages.

sourcePrinciples: QUO is a future Phone / Voice / Text sensory portal.
CRM - New Signals remains the source of truth for consented follow-up inquiries and approved new work.
Message-only Sona calls remain in QUO's in-app message handling and do not create CRM rows.
Teams remains the internal attention surface.
The M365 Interaction Agent produces evidence and supervised suggestions only.
Outbound behavior remains blocked until a later explicit G3/R3 decision.
This repo does not become the production GAIL OS connector in B10.

eventTaxonomy.notes: Default handling is QUO's in-app message path; CRM is gated by CreateCrmSignal.

eventTaxonomy.retentionNote: Store only a short summary until transcript/recording retention is approved.
Short preview only until raw message retention is approved.

eventTaxonomy.notes: Create a CRM signal only when Sona outputs CreateCrmSignal: true.
Relationship enrichment only until CRM/Graphify matching rules exist.

dedupe.fallbackKeyPattern: QUO:<sourceEventType>:<sourceConversationId or call/thread id>:<normalized caller/sender>:<eventOccurredAt UTC 15-minute bucket>

replayBehavior.zeroMatches: Future approved ingress may create one CRM - New Signals item.
replayBehavior.oneMatch: Map to the existing CRM item and do not create a duplicate.
replayBehavior.sameKeyChangedPayload: Record as possible update/conflict, not a new signal.

fields.CreateCrmSignal: Hard gate from the Sona prompt. Only true may create or match a CRM row.
fields.Title: QUO <SignalType> - <caller/name placeholder> - <event date/time>
fields.SignalType: Phone, Voicemail, SMS, Call Summary, or another approved existing value.
fields.Priority: High only when the caller explicitly states urgency, a deadline, or same-day need; Normal otherwise.
fields.ConsentToFollowUpSystem: Preserve in SourceText or a future dedicated consent field.
fields.IntakeDisposition: Preserve in SourceText, NeedSummary, or a future category field for triage.
fields.InterestArea: Preserve in SourceText, NeedSummary, or a future interest/category field.
fields.PreferredContactMethod: Preserve in NextAction or a future contact preference field.
fields.SourceText: Sanitized metadata block with event id, event class, conversation id, business number label, redacted caller/sender, timestamp, digest, link refs, and outbound blocked note.
fields.RelatedLink: QUO conversation/call/voicemail link only when approved.
fields.NextAction: Human triage note; no outbound reply instruction unless separately approved.

crmMapping.contactUpdateRule: Do not create work by default. Retain as future relationship evidence only after relationship-enrichment rules exist.

quoPlacement: Paste the Sona prompt into the missed-call Sona node during business hours.
Paste the same Sona prompt into the after-hours Sona node.
Leave message-only calls in QUO in-app handling when Sona outputs CreateCrmSignal: false.
If QUO has a separate post-call summary field, use the structured CRM handoff labels from the prompt doc there.

sharePointPlacement.path: Operations Cockpit -> CRM Command Center -> QUO Intake -> CRM - New Signals

blocked: Outbound SMS, callback, reply, email send, or QUO API send.
Raw transcript, full SMS body, webhook secret, API key, or QUO admin configuration exposure in SharePoint.
crmIntakePromptPlacement.futureIngressRule: B10c.1+ should normalize QUO/Sona output through the approved ingress path, then create or match exactly one CRM - New Signals item with IntakeSource = QUO only when CreateCrmSignal is true. CreateCrmSignal false must remain message-only and must not write CRM.

evidenceAndPrivacy.rawPayload: Not present in B10b. B10c/later approval required before local private retention.
evidenceAndPrivacy.fullSmsBody: Not present in B10b. Retention/redaction approval required.
evidenceAndPrivacy.voicemailTranscript: Not present in B10b. Retention/redaction approval required.
evidenceAndPrivacy.recordingLink: Placeholder only in B10b. Access and retention approval required.
evidenceAndPrivacy.phoneNumbers: Fake 555-01xx placeholders only in B10b examples.
evidenceAndPrivacy.committedEvidenceRule: Sanitized summaries, digests, ids, and proof outcomes only.

b10cApprovalChecklist: Exact QUO business intake number(s) or internal test number(s).
Exact secret/signature storage location and revoke path.
Exact raw payload evidence location and retention/redaction rule.
Dedupe/idempotency rule for the selected event class.
Named owner and visible disable/pause path.
Confirmation that outbound SMS, callback, reply, and QUO API send remain blocked.
Confirmation that message-only calls remain in QUO and do not write CRM.
Confirmation that the event routes through CRM - New Signals.

syntheticFixtures.label: missed_call_fake
syntheticFixtures.messageOrSummary: Fake missed call fixture. No real QUO payload.

syntheticFixtures.label: inbound_sms_fake
syntheticFixtures.messageOrSummary: Fake SMS preview. No real client text.

syntheticFixtures.label: contact_update_fake
syntheticFixtures.messageOrSummary: Fake contact update fixture. Relationship evidence only.

acceptance: Normalized source event contract is defined.
CRM mapping into CRM - New Signals is defined.
Raw payload retention, redaction, and evidence expectations are defined.
Visible approval, disable, revoke, and owner paths are defined.
Synthetic fixtures use fake data only.
No live QUO or M365 action occurred during B10b.
